reviews.title: Helpful plan with a few things to watch for
reviews.text:
I bought the 3-Year Standard Geek Squad Protection for my treadmill. I’ve used this plan before with other workout equipment, and it really has helped me in the past. Best Buy has always taken care of the repair or replacement once the plan is active, which gives me peace of mind since I use my treadmill every day.

There are a few things to keep in mind. Even though you buy a 3-year plan, it doesn’t start until the manufacturer’s warranty ends. I had a treadmill in the past with very poor support from the manufacturer, and Best Buy couldn’t step in until that warranty was over. That part was frustrating because I felt stuck for a while.

Once the Geek Squad plan kicked in, I still had some trouble getting clear answers. I spoke with many different customer service reps, and a lot of them gave me different information. It took a while before I reached someone who understood the issue and helped me move forward. When the right person steps in, the service is great, but getting there can take patience.

One more thing to note is that once you use the protection plan for a replacement, the plan is considered fulfilled. If that happens early in the coverage period, you lose the remaining time.

Even with those challenges, I still feel the plan has good value, especially for fitness equipment that takes daily wear and tear. I would recommend it, as long as you know what to expect.

reviews.title: Extremely long hold and not actually 3 yr coverage
reviews.text: I purchased the 3 year Geek Squad plan. When I called to get a new exercise bike repaired, I was told I needed to contact Nautilus. I spent over an hour on hold with the Geek Squad to be told this. They said they wouldn't cover it until the year warranty with Nautilus expired. I said does that mean that I paid for three years, but really only get two years? The representative said yes. Just be aware that this is the case. I have used Geek squad for a washer before and they were amazing. This has not been my experience with the exercise bike.
reviews.author: Constance

reviews.title: Great bike. Just a few nit picks
reviews.text:
We've had this bike for about a week so still learning to use it. It's not perfect but nothing ever. Overall we really like it so far. The only hiccup was a missing part. Customer support was very nice but informed me it was gong to take six weeks for them to get the part to us. It was a cosmetic piece so we can use the bike but six weeks to replace a part someone forgot to put in the box?

PROS
--------
-The price is great
-Solid construction
-Built in speakers
-Comfortable seat
-The Explore the World app synch easily and gives you six free courses with beautiful scenery
- Very Quiet
- Smooth operation

CONS
---------
-USB port charges a phone but not enough amps to charge an iPad
-The bike has bluetooth out but not in so the built in speakers are not bluetooth capable
-The USB charging port does not support audio so you need to use two cords if you want to play audio and charge at the same time - minor but why?
-The seat is adjustable but slides forward very forcefully when unlocked. It takes a good amount of force to push it back up and lock it in place. Smaller people might have a little trouble with that. It really could use a spring or a piston to make adjustments easier and smoother.
- Unable to synch with apps like Zwift or Rouvy but might be able to if a cadence sensor is purchased separately and attached to the pedals
- The Explore the World app is nice but has very limited on screen info. Just distance and time but not cadence or resistance and the lower LCD screen that is still visible when an iPad is in the holder can not display cadence and resistance at the same time. They should fix one or the other.
- The seat back is hard plastic with a strong lumbar curve. This can be uncomfortable if its not a good fit for your shape and size. It would be great if they offered a mesh back as an option.

Overall its a great bike .The Cons I listed are mostly minor things and most of them are things they could fix pretty easily and inexpensively if they wanted to. The sliding seat is something that would require a bit of a redesign but they should look into that with the next upgrade because if you have multiple people using the bike and some are less muscular this could be annoying if not slightly hazardous.

reviews.author: HighQualityBestBuy

reviews.title: highly recommend! simple and effective treadmill
reviews.text: This ProForm Carbon T7 Treadmill is exactly what I needed in our home gym to allow for an intense workout when I need it and to also allow for uphill walking while I read or catch up on my favorite shows. The iFit platform has been a hit in our house as well, the coached runs add a dynamic I wouldn’t have had otherwise, and we can track our fitness activity using our accounts on the treadmill and on our ProForm bike. For more intense workouts on the treadmill, I find the cushioning to be more forgiving than running on the road, and more comfortable than other treadmills I’ve owned in the past. As a Physical Therapist, I am always aware of the level of impact throughout a week of exercise, and I feel that I can comfortably log more miles when using the Carbon T7. The incline feature has been nice, this allows me to dial back speed and still get a decent workout. My husband has been walking at an incline while watching lectures or when in long video conference meetings. And as I mentioned, the iFit has been a nice added bonus that I did not expect to really utilize, for both the added instruction and engagement, and also for the cumulative stats that we can track between our two ProForm pieces of equipment. Assembly was straightforward, as instructed the assembly does require two people and can be completed in approx. one hour and it's fairly heavy so two people is a must. The treadmill folds up if you need to clear up space, it has a nice locking mechanism so you can easily fold it up and move it around. Thus far the product has been great. I was given this product in exchange for my honest and unbiased opinion, and I will certainly recommend this to my patients who are looking for a home treadmill based on the simplicity of operation, incline function, speed variability, comfort, and for the iFit that is included.
